mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions through lower discount rates and risk premia, raising S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate has moved down for several consecutive observations and weakens if inflation is simultaneously accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS over any 3-year rolling window, or if the sign is negative in more than 55% of easing instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and tighter labor market conditions, which depress equity earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is most visible when unemployment rises from a low base rather than from already elevated levels.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE rises are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates stronger payroll income and retail spending, which supports near-term earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. This mechanism requires both labor income growth and consumer demand growth because either series alone can be noisy.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not outperform single-series signals, or if positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-improvement months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO reflects a slowdown in industrial production and cyclical manufacturing demand, which foreshadows weaker corporate revenue growth and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The signal is strongest when industrial output is already below its recent trend rather than merely volatile.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average and remains there for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend INDPRO episodes do not precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns on average, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55% across out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation runs above the recent trend while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates become more negative and the market interprets policy as behind the curve, which compresses equity multiples and hurts near-term S&P 500 returns. The mechanism fails if inflation pressure is absent or if the policy rate rises quickly enough to offset it.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration episodes with low FEDFUNDS do not produce negative 5-day returns more often than chance, or if the average 5-day return is not materially below zero in triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals a cooling housing cycle that usually precedes slower consumer demand, weaker construction activity, and lower equity returns over the next quarter. The effect is delayed because housing acts through spending and credit channels rather than immediate earnings revisions.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by at least 10% versus its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drawdowns are followed by non-negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in most cases, or if the sign of the average forward return is positive across two or more expansion cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth signals a broad demand deceleration that should reduce expected corporate revenue and push S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The joint condition matters because one series can weaken for statistical or seasonal reasons without confirming a true demand shock.
failure_condition: Reject if joint low-growth readings in GDPC1 and RSAFS are not associated with weaker 20-day returns than either series alone, or if the conditional median forward return is not negative in recession-adjacent periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor market, a rising UNRATE paired with weakening PAYEMS indicates job losses are spreading beyond noise and will pressure consumer spending and equity earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is regime-dependent because the same unemployment increase is less informative early in expansions.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises while PAYEMS growth turns negative on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if the joint unemployment-and-payroll deterioration does not predict negative 20-day returns better than baseline, or if triggered cases produce positive returns in more than 40% of observations.
